title: Business Class Email Overview
summaryWhat: Product overview document covering Business Class Email — a secure, domain-name-based email solution with quick pitch, top-line benefits, features, and messaging pillars.
summaryWhy: Gives reps and partners a structured narrative for positioning branded email as a trust-builder versus free @gmail.com addresses, and serves as the source-of-truth messaging for all downstream email content.
summaryHow: Use as the master messaging doc for discovery calls, as raw material for landing-page copy and proposals, and as onboarding reading for new reps selling the email product line.

title: Custom Website Development Overview
summaryWhat: Source-of-truth overview for Custom Website Development — built-from-scratch WordPress sites, owned outright by the customer, starting at $3,500. Includes a dedicated 'When to recommend' page that explicitly tells reps when to downgrade to DIFM.
summaryWhy: Refreshes the 2023 Custom Website overview with explicit pairing guidance against Website Design (Product #6). Most prospects who ask for custom actually need DIFM — this doc helps reps qualify correctly.
summaryHow: Use alongside the DIFM overview to qualify the right customer to the right website product. Ownership-sensitive, brand-led, or feature-rich businesses go custom; everyone else goes DIFM.

title: Domain Registration Overview
summaryWhat: Overview of the Domain Registration service — TLD selection, auto-renewal, transfers, domain privacy, and how a domain anchors a small business's online identity.
summaryWhy: Helps partners position domains as the first building block of an SMB's online presence, and the gateway product that leads into email, hosting, and website sales.
summaryHow: Use as the primary reference for domain messaging across sales and marketing, to feed landing-page and blog copy, and to train reps on TLD positioning by market.

title: Email Marketing / Announcer Pro Overview
summaryWhat: Overview of Announcer Pro — a drag-and-drop email marketing platform for SMBs with templates, campaign scheduling, and audience segmentation.
summaryWhy: Gives partners a way to position email marketing as a retention-and-reengagement play that grows customer lifetime value, not just an acquisition channel.
summaryHow: Use to brief reps on Announcer Pro positioning, to source quick-pitch lines for outreach, and as the spine of the Announcer Pro sales deck and landing page.

title: OneList Plus Overview
summaryWhat: Overview of OneList Plus — a directory listings and local SEO product that syndicates a business's NAP data across 7 to 40+ directories with lock-and-monitor protection.
summaryWhy: Positions OneList Plus as the answer to SMB local-search visibility, anchoring the 'Get Found' journey and bridging into Reviews Promoter for the full reputation stack.
summaryHow: Use as the core messaging doc for directory listings, to train reps on local SEO value, and as source material for OneList Plus decks, one-pagers, and blog content.

title: Online Fax Overview
summaryWhat: Source-of-truth overview for the Online Fax product family — Standard, Encrypted (HIPAA-compliant), and Multi-User tiers. 11 file formats, 99.9% uptime, toll-free number included.
summaryWhy: Equips reps to position fax as still-mandatory in healthcare / legal / real estate, while replacing the costly physical machine. Encrypted tier opens a high-margin HIPAA conversation.
summaryHow: Use as the master fax messaging doc, the reference for vertical-specific outreach (healthcare/legal), and onboarding material for any partner serving regulated industries.

title: Online Presence Builder Overview
summaryWhat: Overview of Online Presence Builder — Hostopia's DIY drag-and-drop website builder with mobile-optimized templates, design themes, hosting, and add-ons for SMBs.
summaryWhy: Positions the DIY builder as the approachable entry point for SMBs who want a professional site without designer costs, and the platform that unlocks add-ons like Ecommerce, Appointment Scheduler, and Gift Certificates.
summaryHow: Use as the messaging spine for the DIY website product, to brief reps on positioning DIY vs. DIFM vs. Custom, and to source copy for the OPB deck, landing pages, and onboarding content.

title: Ecommerce Overview
summaryWhat: Source-of-truth overview for the Ecommerce product family across four tiers — Sell 12, Sell 100, Sell 1K, Sell Unlimited. Zero transaction fees, PCI-DSS Level 1, 40+ payment gateways, real-time shipping.
summaryWhy: Refreshes 2023 stats with current 2025 e-commerce data (76% / 65% / 60%) and the four-tier plan structure. The 'zero transaction fees' angle is the strongest competitive wedge versus Shopify / BigCommerce.
summaryHow: Use as the master e-commerce messaging doc, the reference for landing-page and ad campaigns, and as raw material for partner enablement on the e-commerce product.

title: Professional (DIFM) Website Design Overview
summaryWhat: Overview of the Professional Do-It-For-Me website design service — 1-on-1 consultation, up to 10-page designs, monthly maintenance, and bundled domain-based email.
summaryWhy: Positions DIFM as the middle-tier option between DIY Online Presence Builder and full Custom Design, targeting SMBs who want expert-built sites with ongoing support.
summaryHow: Use as the messaging doc for DIFM positioning, to train reps on the DIY vs. DIFM vs. Custom tiering, and as source copy for the DIFM deck, landing pages, and upsell scripts.

title: Professional Logo Design Overview
summaryWhat: Source-of-truth overview for Professional Logo Design — a human-designed logo service delivered through a dedicated Logo Design Coordinator, with 11 final file formats and full ownership of the final mark.
summaryWhy: Anchors all logo-related sales and marketing messaging in one document, reflecting current 2025 stats and the three-tier (Bronze/Silver/Gold) plan structure used across every channel.
summaryHow: Use as the master messaging doc for discovery calls, landing-page copy, proposals, and rep onboarding when selling logo design.

title: Reviews Promoter Overview
summaryWhat: Overview of Reviews Promoter — an AI-driven reputation management platform that solicits reviews via email/SMS, auto-posts to social, and embeds testimonials on customer websites.
summaryWhy: Positions reputation management as a trust-and-SEO play, pairing with OneList Plus to complete the 'Get Found' bundle for SMBs competing locally.
summaryHow: Use as the core messaging doc for Reviews Promoter, to train reps on AI-assisted review requests, and as source material for the Reviews Promoter deck, infographics, and landing pages.

title: SSL Certificate Overview
summaryWhat: Overview of SSL certificates — encryption, HTTPS, the browser security icon, SEO benefits, and why modern websites require SSL as table stakes.
summaryWhy: Positions SSL as mandatory infrastructure (not optional) for any SMB website, giving partners an easy attach product on every website and ecommerce sale.
summaryHow: Use as the messaging doc for SSL attach conversations, to train reps on why SSL is a must-have, and as source copy for SSL decks, infographics, and checkout-flow marketing.

title: Custom Website Development Sales Deck
summaryWhat: Sales deck for Custom Website Development covering features, benefits, objection handling, and the value of a fully tailored WordPress-hosted site.
summaryWhy: Gives reps a turnkey visual tool to walk SMB prospects through premium web design positioning and close against DIY alternatives.
summaryHow: Use on discovery and first-pitch calls with SMBs evaluating web design options, particularly prospects needing custom functionality or unique branding.

title: Professional (DIFM) Website Design Sales Deck
summaryWhat: Sales deck for Professional DIFM website design opening with '98% of consumers used the internet to find local businesses' (BrightLocal 2023) and walking through the DIFM process and plan options.
summaryWhy: Equips reps to pitch the middle-tier web design offer to SMBs who want expert help without full Custom pricing.
summaryHow: Use on website-focused sales calls with SMBs who lack technical capacity, and as the anchor visual when pitching monthly-maintenance DIFM plans.

title: Domains & Email Sales Deck

summaryWhy: Gives reps a bundled domain+email pitch that positions branded communications as the trust-and-rapport foundation of an SMB's online identity.
summaryHow: Use on discovery calls with SMBs still on free email providers, and as the anchor deck for domain+email attach conversations after logo or website sales.
